title:
Module 8
Account Safety & Precautions
subtitle: Learn How to Protect Your Social Media Accounts from Bans and What to Do if It Happens
output_filename: Module 8 - Account Safety & Precautions/Module 8 - Account Safety & Precautions.pdf

blocks.content: There are two types of bans: visible bans and invisible bans. Visible bans are when the platform tells you directly. Invisible bans (shadowbans) are far more dangerous because you will not know it happened until your growth flatlines. Understanding both is critical to protecting your accounts.

blocks.title: The 2025 Instagram AI Moderation Shift
blocks.content: In 2025, Meta deployed LLaMA-based AI moderation that fundamentally changed the landscape. This system is hyper-sensitive to AI-generated content and caused a mass ban wave from May through August 2025. Key implications: Instagram actively flags AI-generated images, IMEI/device bans can affect ALL accounts linked to a banned phone, and false positives are common (even legitimate accounts get caught). This is not going away — it is the new normal. Every safety habit in this module exists because of this reality.

items: Content removal — single post taken down (warning level)
Temporary restriction — limited features for 24-72 hours
Shadowban — content stops being shown to non-followers (invisible, most dangerous)
Account suspension — temporary lockout requiring appeal
Permanent ban — account deleted, usually not recoverable
Device/IMEI ban — ALL accounts on that device are affected (Instagram-specific, post-2025)
Posting suggestive content too early (no trust built)
Aggressive follow/unfollow behavior (bot-like patterns)
Spam-like posting patterns (too much, too fast)
Link sharing during warmup phase
Multiple accounts on same device/IP behaving identically
Reports from other users
AI-generated content detected by platform moderation
Using banned hashtags on Instagram
Third-party apps that require Instagram login

right.content: A shadowban will NOT show a notification. Your content simply stops reaching new people. The only way to detect it is by monitoring your metrics daily.

items: Instagram: Check Insights — if 'reach from non-followers' drops to near zero, you are likely shadowbanned
Instagram: Check Account Status in Settings → Account → Account Status weekly for any flags
TikTok: If views suddenly drop from thousands to double digits, check notifications for content violations
Twitter/X: Check account health at shadowban.yuzurisa.com — warmup continues until ALL indicators are green
Reddit: If your posts get zero upvotes consistently in subreddits where they used to perform, you may be site-wide shadowbanned

right.title: IMAGE PLACEHOLDER
right.content: Instagram Insights comparison: Left shows healthy reach (steady 'reach from non-followers' graph). Right shows shadowbanned reach (graph drops to near zero). Annotate the difference clearly.

right.title: Early Detection Rule
right.content: By the time you notice a shadowban, it has usually been active for days. Check your metrics every single day. Early detection means faster recovery. Late detection means weeks of lost growth.

blocks.content: Prevention is infinitely easier than recovery. These daily habits keep your accounts healthy and your algorithmic trust high. Treat these as non-negotiable rules, not suggestions. Every operator who has lost an account wishes they had been more disciplined with these habits.

items: Scroll your feed for 10-15 minutes before posting anything
Like and comment on other creators' posts genuinely
Reply to every comment on your own posts (engagement signal)
Watch Stories and Reels from accounts you follow
Keep follow/unfollow activity low — max 10-15 per day
Post at consistent times each day (algorithm learns your schedule)
Never push content boundaries on Instagram or TikTok
Keep swimwear/lingerie content to absolute minimum on strict platforms
No sexual captions, no suggestive language, no innuendo
If unsure whether content is safe, do not post it — always err on caution
Instagram is your revenue engine — protect it above everything
Always add 2% grain to AI images before posting (Module 3)

left.content: If an account gets banned, you lose all posted content. A proper backup strategy prevents losing weeks of work.

items: Keep every posted image in your /Posted/ archive folder
Save all caption text in a separate document
Screenshot your best-performing posts and their metrics
Back up your content library to cloud storage (Google Drive, Dropbox)
Never rely on platform storage — always have local copies of everything
Never switch between accounts rapidly on the same device
Log out of personal accounts before using persona accounts
Keep VPN consistent — do not jump between locations mid-session
Do not use third-party apps that require Instagram login
Avoid automation tools that violate platform ToS
Keep your phone's OS and apps updated at all times
Instagram: Engage 10 minutes before AND after posting. Captions typed manually, never copy-pasted. Stay under 150 likes/hour, 60 comments/hour, 60 follows/hour
TikTok: Browse FYP at least 20 minutes daily. Wait 4+ hours between posts. Never batch upload. Captions typed manually
Twitter/X: Each account must use one device and one IP. Enable 2FA. Mark media as sensitive if needed. Check shadowban status weekly
Reddit: Never post links or promotions until karma is built. All comments must sound human and non-promotional. Avoid ChatGPT-sounding responses
Reach from non-followers (should be steady or growing)
Engagement rate per post (likes + comments / followers)
Story views (should be 10-30% of follower count)
Profile visits (should be steady or growing)
Any warning notifications from the platform
Instagram Account Status (Settings → Account → Account Status)

right.title: IMAGE PLACEHOLDER
right.content: Daily safety checklist graphic: Morning routine showing timeline of trust signals, content rules, and metrics to monitor in a clean, scannable checklist format.

sections.header: Lesson 3: What to Do If You Get Banned

blocks.content: Despite best practices, bans happen. They are part of operating in this space. What separates winners from quitters is how fast and smart you respond. This lesson covers exact steps for each scenario, including appeal templates you can use immediately.

items: STOP posting immediately — do not try to 'post through it'
Reduce all activity to passive scrolling only for 48-72 hours
Remove any recently posted content that may have triggered it
Check if you violated any content guidelines recently
After 48-72 hours, resume posting slowly (50% of normal frequency)
Monitor reach metrics daily — shadowbans typically lift in 7-14 days
If reach does not recover after 14 days, the account may be permanently suppressed
Submit an appeal immediately through the platform's process
Be polite, professional, and brief in your appeal
Do not create a new account on the same device/IP while suspended
Wait for the appeal — most platforms respond within 24-72 hours
If first appeal fails, wait 30 days and try again with different wording

left.content:
Subject: Account Review Request

Hi Instagram team,

My account @[username] was recently restricted/suspended. I believe this may have been flagged in error. I always try to follow community guidelines and post appropriate content.

I would really appreciate it if you could review my account. I am happy to remove any content that may have caused an issue.

Thank you for your time.
[Name]

Hi TikTok team,

My account @[username] was restricted. I believe this was a mistake. I follow community guidelines and only post appropriate content. Could you please review my account?

Thank you.
[Name]

left.title: Appeal Strategy
left.content: Appeals should be short, polite, and non-confrontational. Never argue, never threaten, never mention other accounts. Sound like a confused normal user, not an angry marketer. This tone has the highest success rate.

items: Accept the loss — do not waste time on repeated appeals (one attempt is fine)
Analyze what went wrong so you do not repeat the mistake
Start fresh with a NEW persona (different look, name, niche)
Use a completely new device, phone number, and IP address
Do NOT try to recreate the same persona — platforms detect this
Follow the warmup protocol from Module 5 with extra caution
Your content library and systems still exist — rebuilding is faster than starting from zero

right.content: Since 2025, Instagram can ban at the device level. This means every account on that phone is compromised. If you suspect a device ban:

items: Do NOT create any new accounts on that device
All existing accounts on that device should be considered at risk
You need a completely new phone (factory reset alone may not work)
New SIM, new IP, new email — everything must be fresh
Consider using a cloud phone solution (GeeLark) to avoid future device bans

right.title: Recovery Timeline
right.content:
Shadowban: 7-14 days of reduced activity, then gradual return.
Temporary suspension: 24-72 hours for appeal response.
Permanent ban: Full restart required — new device, new persona, 21-28 day warmup.
Device ban: New phone required.

A ban is not the end. It is a setback. If your system works, you can rebuild. The knowledge, content pipeline, and processes you have built do not disappear because one account does. Experienced operators budget for account losses — they always have backup accounts warming up.

items: Always have at least one backup account warming up on a separate device
Keep your content library backed up locally and in the cloud
Document your best-performing strategies so you can replicate them quickly
Build your system to be account-independent — the system survives any single ban
The operators making real money have been banned multiple times — they just rebuild faster each time

right.title: Mindset
right.content: Getting banned is not a sign of failure. It is a sign that you are operating in a competitive space. The winners are the ones who prepare for bans, respond quickly, and rebuild smarter. The quitters are the ones who take it personally.

blocks.content: Running multiple accounts is the highest-risk activity in this business. Platforms are designed to detect multi-account operations. One mistake can cascade and take down all your accounts simultaneously. These rules are absolute and non-negotiable.

items: One phone per persona (ideal and safest)
Max 2 accounts per device (absolute maximum — still risky)
TikTok: max 3 accounts per device
Never log into two personas in the same app session
Never switch between accounts rapidly — wait 30+ minutes between logins
Factory reset devices completely if reusing hardware for a new persona
Each persona should use a different IP address
Use mobile data (different SIM) or separate VPN/proxy profiles
Never access two personas from the same WiFi network at the same time
Keep VPN locations consistent per persona — do not jump between cities
Residential or mobile proxies are safest for multi-account operations
Ensure timezone matches your IP geolocation (timezone mismatch is a detection signal)
Each persona gets a unique email address
Each persona gets a unique phone number
Never cross-follow between your own accounts
Never share links between your own accounts
Never mention one persona from another
Different content niches, posting schedules, and caption styles
Same IP address across accounts (most common cause)
Same device fingerprint (IMEI, browser fingerprint, etc.)
Cross-following between your own accounts
Identical posting patterns (same times, same hashtags, same caption style)
Shared link-in-bio URLs across accounts
Logging into multiple accounts from one device in rapid succession
Synchronized activity patterns (all accounts post at the same time daily)

right.title: Nuclear Rule
right.content: If one account gets banned, do NOT access your other accounts from the same device or IP for at least 72 hours. A ban investigation can cascade to linked accounts. Quarantine immediately. This is the single most important multi-account safety rule.

items: Check Instagram Account Status (Settings → Account → Account Status) weekly
Check Twitter/X shadowban status weekly using shadowban.yuzurisa.com
Monitor reach metrics on all platforms daily — any sudden drop signals trouble
Set calendar reminders for weekly health checks on all accounts
Keep a log of any platform warnings or content removals per account

summary_points: Instagram's 2025 AI moderation shift permanently changed the landscape — prepare for flagging and have backups
Shadowbans are invisible — monitor reach from non-followers daily to detect them early
Prevention beats recovery: daily trust signals, safe content, and platform rate limits keep accounts healthy
If shadowbanned: stop posting, go passive for 48-72 hours, resume at 50% frequency
If permanently banned: start fresh with new device, new persona, new everything — rebuild smarter
Device/IMEI bans affect ALL accounts on that phone — you may need a completely new device
Always keep backup accounts warming up and content backed up locally and in the cloud
Multi-account safety requires complete separation: device, IP, email, phone number — no exceptions
If one account gets banned, quarantine all other accounts for 72 hours minimum
Use appeal templates that are short, polite, and non-confrontational — never argue or threaten
